title: CloudNativePG
description: CloudNativePG cluster health from the native cnpg_* instance metrics: connections, throughput, cache, replication, WAL & archiving, checkpoints, storage, and the metrics collector itself.

var_datname.name: datname
var_datname.description: Filter per-database panels by database name (datname label).

widgets.title: Cluster Up
widgets.description: 1 if all selected PostgreSQL instances are up. Uses the minimum across pods, so any down instance drops it to 0.

widgets.title: PostgreSQL Version
widgets.description: Major.minor PostgreSQL version reported by the cluster.

widgets.title: Streaming Replicas
widgets.description: Number of streaming replicas connected to the primary.

widgets.title: Total Backends
widgets.description: Total backend connections across selected instances, all states. The Backends by State panel breaks this down.

widgets.title: Longest Transaction
widgets.description: Duration of the longest-running transaction. Long transactions hold locks and block vacuum.

widgets.title: Total DB Size
widgets.description: Sum of all database sizes on the primary.

widgets.title: WAL Archive Age
widgets.description: Seconds since the last successful WAL archival. A rising value means archiving has stalled.

widgets.title: Collection Errors
widgets.description: 1 if the last metrics collection on any instance ended with an error.

widgets.title: Replica Mode
widgets.description: 1 if the cluster is a designated replica cluster (replicating from an external primary), 0 for a standalone primary cluster.

widgets.title: Fencing
widgets.description: 1 if any instance is fenced (isolated from the cluster for maintenance/recovery). Should normally be 0.

widgets.title: Switchover Required
widgets.description: 1 if a manual switchover is required (the operator cannot promote automatically). Should normally be 0.

widgets.title: Nodes Used
widgets.description: Distinct Kubernetes nodes hosting the cluster's instances. A value of 1 with multiple instances means no high-availability node spread.

widgets.title: Backends by State
widgets.description: Backend connections grouped by state (active, idle, idle in transaction, …).

widgets.title: Backends by Database
widgets.description: Backend connections grouped by database.

filter.expression: pod IN $pod AND datname IN $datname AND datname != ''

widgets.title: Backends Waiting on Locks
widgets.description: Backends blocked waiting on other queries. Sustained values point to lock contention.

widgets.title: Longest Transaction by Database
widgets.description: Maximum transaction duration per database.

filter.expression: pod IN $pod AND datname IN $datname AND datname != ''

widgets.title: Transactions /s
widgets.description: Committed and rolled-back transactions per second across selected databases.

widgets.title: Tuple Activity /s
widgets.description: Rows returned, fetched, inserted, updated, and deleted per second.

widgets.title: Deadlocks & Conflicts /s
widgets.description: Deadlocks detected and queries canceled due to recovery conflicts (standby) per second. Any sustained value needs investigation.

filter.expression: pod IN $pod AND datname IN $datname AND datname != ''
pod IN $pod AND datname IN $datname AND datname != ''

widgets.title: Temp File Bytes /s
widgets.description: Temporary file bytes written per second, by database. High values mean queries are spilling to disk (raise work_mem).

filter.expression: pod IN $pod AND datname IN $datname AND datname != ''

widgets.title: Buffer Cache Hit Ratio
widgets.description: Shared-buffer cache hit ratio from blks_hit / (blks_hit + blks_read) * 100. Below ~99% on an OLTP workload suggests undersized shared_buffers.

widgets.title: Block I/O /s
widgets.description: Buffer cache hits vs. disk block reads per second.

widgets.title: Block I/O Time /s
widgets.description: Milliseconds per second spent reading and writing data blocks (requires track_io_timing). Rising read time points to disk pressure.

widgets.title: Temp Files /s
widgets.description: Temporary files created per second, by database.

filter.expression: pod IN $pod AND datname IN $datname AND datname != ''

widgets.title: Replication Lag (standby)
widgets.description: Replica lag behind the primary in seconds, per standby instance.

widgets.title: Write / Flush / Replay Lag (primary view)
widgets.description: Per-replica write, flush, and replay lag in seconds as seen by the primary, grouped by replica application name.

widgets.title: Replication Status
widgets.description: Per-instance recovery state (1 = standby) and WAL receiver up (1 = streaming) flags.

widgets.title: Replication Slot Lag
widgets.description: WAL retained per replication slot in bytes (restart-LSN diff). Growing lag on an inactive slot risks unbounded WAL accumulation.

widgets.title: WAL Directory Size
widgets.description: Total size of the pg_wal directory in bytes, per instance.

widgets.title: WAL Segment Count
widgets.description: Number of WAL segments in pg_wal, per instance.

widgets.title: WAL Archive Status
widgets.description: WAL segments waiting to be archived (ready) vs. archived (done). A growing ready count means archiving is falling behind.

widgets.title: WAL Archiver Ops /s
widgets.description: Successful vs. failed WAL archive operations per second. Any sustained failure rate needs investigation.

widgets.title: Time Since Last Archive / Failure
widgets.description: Seconds since the last successful archival and since the last failure, per instance. The success age sawtooths as WAL is archived; a steadily climbing value means archiving stalled.

widgets.title: Checkpoints /s
widgets.description: Timed (scheduled) vs. requested checkpoints per second. A high requested rate means checkpoints are being forced by WAL volume — consider raising max_wal_size.

widgets.title: Checkpoint Write / Sync Time /s
widgets.description: Milliseconds per second spent writing and syncing buffers during checkpoints.

widgets.title: Buffers Written /s
widgets.description: Buffers written per second by the checkpointer (during checkpoints) and the background writer, plus buffers allocated.

widgets.title: Database Size
widgets.description: Disk space consumed per database.

filter.expression: pod IN $pod AND datname IN $datname AND datname != ''

widgets.title: Transaction ID Age
widgets.description: Transactions since the frozen XID per database. Climbing toward ~2.1 billion risks transaction-ID wraparound — vacuum should keep this well below.

filter.expression: pod IN $pod AND datname IN $datname AND datname != ''

widgets.title: MultiXact ID Age
widgets.description: Multixact age from the frozen multixact ID per database. Like transaction-ID age, climbing values risk multixact wraparound; vacuum keeps it bounded.

filter.expression: pod IN $pod AND datname IN $datname AND datname != ''

widgets.title: Metrics Collection Duration
widgets.description: Time the CNPG exporter takes to collect metrics from PostgreSQL, by collector. Rising duration can indicate database pressure.

widgets.title: Metrics Collections /s
widgets.description: Rate of PostgreSQL metric collection cycles per instance.
